Buddhist temple|image=File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Omuro school|WD=Q420739}}|prefecture={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.505625|N|133.85069444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621649]] Wikidata name: Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yugasan Rendaiji | native_name = {{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} | image = [[File:{{ill|Rendaiji Main Hall|ja|蓮台寺 01|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.jpg|270px]] | image description = Main Hall (Sōhonden) | address = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, Kojima Yuka 2855 | latitude = 34 | latitude minutes = 30 | latitude seconds = 20.25 | longitude = 133 | longitude minutes = 51 | longitude seconds = 2.5 | mountain_name = {{ill|Yuka-yama|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka-yama|1=Yuga Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577570}} | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism, Mimuro-ha|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism, Mimuro-ha|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | temple_Ranking = Classified as an “Extraordinary Head Temple” | main_image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}{{ill|Yogācāra Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yogācāra Daigongen|1=Yuga Daigongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573853}} | founded = Tradition: Year 3 of {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daibō Era}} (703) | founder = | official_name = Yuka-yama Yogācāra Daigongen Rendaiji | alternate_names = | pilgrimage = {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No. 6{{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 8 | cultural_properties = Guest Hall, Tahō Tower, Kikujidō Illustrated Folding Screen, and the Bell (Designated as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property)Copper Sword (Designated as a City Important Cultural Property) | map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Yugasan Rendaiji''' ({{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}}) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Mimuro Sect|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Mimuro Sect|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} located in the Kojima area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is classified as an Extraordinary Head Temple. Its mountain name is Yuka-yama (由加山), and its principal images are the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu and Yogācāra Daigongen. It is pilgrimage site No. 6 on the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and No. 8 on the {{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == Tradition holds that in the 10th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō Era}} (738), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} enshrined the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu (the principal image) along with Yogācāra Daigongen (a manifestation of the trace of Amida and Yakushi) and thus founded the temple. Although the temple declined after being damaged by warfare at the end of the Heian period, it was revived during the Muromachi period by a high-Ranking monk. In the Edo period it received the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Han}} of Bizen Province. Long revered for its Yogācāra Daigongen, following the Meiji [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] edict, a separate shrine – later known as {{ill|Yuka Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} – was established within the temple grounds. After the post–World War II dissolution of State Shinto, although formally separated, the monastic staff continued to perform rituals in the main hall so that, in practice, the pre–Meiji state of affairs was restored. As a result, while Yuka Shrine retained its legal status as a separate religious corporation and maintained its own priests, it was in effect nearly dormant. In 1997 (Heisei 9) on March 3, Yuka Shrine began independent religious activities by occupying part of the temple buildings. Since then Rendaiji and Yuka Shrine have been in conflict—with Rendaiji seeking the return of its buildings—though Yuka Shrine continues its independent operations. A traditional custom called “Ryōmairi” (simultaneous pilgrimage to both shrines) developed in the Edo period in which pilgrims visited both the Yogācāra Daigongen of Yugasan and the {{ill|Kinpirō Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpirō Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現|1=Kompira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167077}} (now at {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kintōhira-gū}}) in Sanuki Province. == Overview == It is widely venerated as a protective deity against misfortune. In 2007 (Heisei 19) a 7.59‑meter tall wooden statue of the immovable 【ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王】—the tallest of its kind in Japan—was completed. The approach (sando) up Yuka-yama is lined by a traditional town (monzenchō) whose local specialty is the sweet rice cake known as {{ill|Ankoro Mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoro Mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}. == Cultural Properties == ; Okayama Prefecture–designated Important Cultural Properties * The Nakazato Fujizuka (mound) – one of the fujizuka built by the Fuji-kō group (pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. In Kurashiki it is one of the larger mounds, with a height of 12 meters from its southern base and a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument dated to 1822 (Bunsei 5) indicates that its construction began in the Edo period. Among 36 stone structures, its dōsojin monument is unique in the district. ; Treasures * Ema (votive plaques) depicting the subjugation of the Yamata no Orochi (dating from Tenpō 5, July) * Ema commemorating a Fuji ascent (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting a pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)＜ref＞{{Cite book|和書|author=Ishikamin Library Local Materials Section|title=Ema of Nerima|publisher=Nerima City Board of Education|year=1978|page=175,178}}＜/ref＞ == Address == * 2855 Kojima Yuka, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Setchu Chūō Expressway}} at {{ill|Mizushima IC|ja|水島IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水島インターチェンジ|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}}, take {{ill|Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja|岡山児島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道21号岡山児島線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472422}} east toward Okayama, and at the “Gōnai” intersection turn east onto {{ill|Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja|玉野福田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道62号玉野福田線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 62|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472679}}. Continue straight along the route – about 15 minutes by car. * By train: Approximately a 15‑minute taxi ride from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ohashi Line}}. * By bus: A “Shimoden” bus (downward bus) runs from Kojima Station; however, there are only two round-trips per day (one in the morning, one in the evening) with a stop time of about 5 minutes. (Buses do not run on Saturdays, Sundays, holidays, or the New Year’s three days.) == Adjacent Sacred Sites == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkai-in|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-in|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} – '''6 Yugasan Rendaiji''' – 7 {{ill|Entsū-ji|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsū-ji|1=Entsū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Kurashiki High School Educational Research Group, Social Studies Division, History Section|title=New Edition: Historical Strolls of Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppan|year=1991|pages=96–97}} * Local explanatory plaques == References == {{Reflist|group=2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuka Shrine Main|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yoshida Yoshi-dō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Yoshi-dō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} – credited with painting the 50‐faced barrier paintings at Rendaiji. == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Yugasan Rendaiji Official Website] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Properties Protection Division] {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] }} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621649]] Wikidata name: Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rendaiji | image = [[File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall | Location = 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture | Latitude = 34|lat_m = 30|lat_s = 20.25 | Longitude = 133|long_m = 51|long_s = 2.5 | Mountain Name = {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}} | Sect = {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | Rank = Special Head Temple | Main image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}
{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} | Founded Year = Traditionally {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 10) | Founder = Traditionally {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} | Formal Name = Yuga-san Yuga Daigongen Rendaiji | Cultural Properties = Guest Hall, Tahōtō Pagoda, Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen, Bonshō (Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture)
Bronze Swords (Important Cultural Property of Kurashiki City) | Map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Rendaiji''' (蓮台寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, classified as a Special Head Temple. Its mountain name is {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The main images are {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}} and {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}. It is the 6th temple in the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and the 8th in the {{ill|108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == According to tradition, Rendaiji was founded in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} (Tenpyō 10) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, who enshrined Eleven-Faced Kannon as the main deity alongside {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}, manifestations of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}}.[https://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html History of Yuga-san Rendaiji] The temple declined after being ravaged by fire in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, but it was restored during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}} by the monk Zōun. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it was under the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} in {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}. {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} had been a focal point of faith for centuries, but the temple was divided following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} policy of the early {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治初年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}}, with the grounds allocated to form {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}} (later Yuga Shrine Main Shrine). After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}} and the dissolution of State Shinto, the temple and shrine operated de facto as a single entity, although formally separate. In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} (Heisei 9), disputes arose when Yuga Shrine resumed independent religious activities, leading to a legal battle over property rights. These conflicts were attributed to disagreements between the head priest of Rendaiji and his brother, the chief priest of Yuga Shrine. The custom of {{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—visiting both Yuga Daigongen and {{ill|Kotohira-gū|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}})—was popular during the Edo Period. == Overview == Rendaiji is revered as a temple for warding off misfortunes. In {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} (Heisei 19), Japan's tallest wooden statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} at 7.59 meters was completed.[https://yugasan.jp/19nen.html Yuga-san Rendaiji - Yakuyoke Fudō] The a11roach to Yuga Mountain is lined with a {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monzen-machi|qq=}}, with {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} as its specialty. == Cultural Properties == * '''Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture''' ''' Guest Hall ''' Tahōtō Pagoda ''' Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5428.htm Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen - Kurashiki City] - Stylistically resembling the works of {{ill|Kaihō Yūshō|en|Kaihō Yūshō|WD=Q3191951|lt=Kaihō Yūshō}} and {{ill|Kanō Sanraku|en|Kanō Sanraku|WD=Q3064625|lt=Kanō Sanraku}}, this screen showcases the vigor of pine trees and rock faces. ''' Bonshō (Temple Bell) * '''Important Cultural Properties of Kurashiki City''' ''' Bronze Swords == Location == * 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From {{ill|Mizushima Interchange|ja|水島インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima Interchange|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}} on the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Seto Chūō Expressway}}, follow Prefectural Route 21 (Okayama-Kojima Line) to the "Gōnai" intersection, then continue east on Prefectural Route 62. A11rox. 15 minutes. * By taxi: A11rox. 15 minutes from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ōhashi Line}}. * By bus: Limited bus service from Kojima Station to Yuga Mountain; not recommended for sightseeing due to infrequent schedules. == Pilgrimage Adjacency == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkaiin|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaiin|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} -* '''6 Rendaiji''' -- 7 {{ill|Entsūji (Kurashiki)|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsūji (Kurashiki)|1=Entsūji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == References == * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Society, History Division, ed. ''New Edition: History Walks of Okayama Prefecture.'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, 11. 96-97. * On-site informational panels. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki, Hayashi)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki, Hayashi)|1=Kumano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568940}} * {{ill|Shibata Gidō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Gidō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} - Painted 50 sliding door murals at Rendaiji. == External Links == {{Commons|Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Official site of Yuga-san Rendaiji] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Heritage Protection Section] {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{wikidata link|Q11621649}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Gyōki|伝]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu Shūgō]] [[Category:Temples of the Shingon Omuro branch]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuga faith]]

Buddhist temple|image=File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Omuro school|WD=Q420739}}|prefecture={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.505625|N|133.85069444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621649]] Wikidata name: Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yugasan Rendaiji | native_name = {{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} | image = [[File:{{ill|Rendaiji Main Hall|ja|蓮台寺 01|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.jpg|270px]] | image description = Main Hall (Sōhonden) | address = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, Kojima Yuka 2855 | latitude = 34 | latitude minutes = 30 | latitude seconds = 20.25 | longitude = 133 | longitude minutes = 51 | longitude seconds = 2.5 | mountain_name = {{ill|Yuka-yama|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka-yama|1=Yuga Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577570}} | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism, Mimuro-ha|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism, Mimuro-ha|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | temple_Ranking = Classified as an “Extraordinary Head Temple” | main_image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}{{ill|Yogācāra Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yogācāra Daigongen|1=Yuga Daigongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573853}} | founded = Tradition: Year 3 of {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daibō Era}} (703) | founder = | official_name = Yuka-yama Yogācāra Daigongen Rendaiji | alternate_names = | pilgrimage = {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No. 6{{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 8 | cultural_properties = Guest Hall, Tahō Tower, Kikujidō Illustrated Folding Screen, and the Bell (Designated as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property)Copper Sword (Designated as a City Important Cultural Property) | map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Yugasan Rendaiji''' ({{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}}) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Mimuro Sect|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Mimuro Sect|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} located in the Kojima area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is classified as an Extraordinary Head Temple. Its mountain name is Yuka-yama (由加山), and its principal images are the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu and Yogācāra Daigongen. It is pilgrimage site No. 6 on the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and No. 8 on the {{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == Tradition holds that in the 10th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō Era}} (738), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} enshrined the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu (the principal image) along with Yogācāra Daigongen (a manifestation of the trace of Amida and Yakushi) and thus founded the temple. Although the temple declined after being damaged by warfare at the end of the Heian period, it was revived during the Muromachi period by a high-Ranking monk. In the Edo period it received the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Han}} of Bizen Province. Long revered for its Yogācāra Daigongen, following the Meiji [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] edict, a separate shrine – later known as {{ill|Yuka Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} – was established within the temple grounds. After the post–World War II dissolution of State Shinto, although formally separated, the monastic staff continued to perform rituals in the main hall so that, in practice, the pre–Meiji state of affairs was restored. As a result, while Yuka Shrine retained its legal status as a separate religious corporation and maintained its own priests, it was in effect nearly dormant. In 1997 (Heisei 9) on March 3, Yuka Shrine began independent religious activities by occupying part of the temple buildings. Since then Rendaiji and Yuka Shrine have been in conflict—with Rendaiji seeking the return of its buildings—though Yuka Shrine continues its independent operations. A traditional custom called “Ryōmairi” (simultaneous pilgrimage to both shrines) developed in the Edo period in which pilgrims visited both the Yogācāra Daigongen of Yugasan and the {{ill|Kinpirō Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpirō Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現|1=Kompira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167077}} (now at {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kintōhira-gū}}) in Sanuki Province. == Overview == It is widely venerated as a protective deity against misfortune. In 2007 (Heisei 19) a 7.59‑meter tall wooden statue of the immovable 【ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王】—the tallest of its kind in Japan—was completed. The approach (sando) up Yuka-yama is lined by a traditional town (monzenchō) whose local specialty is the sweet rice cake known as {{ill|Ankoro Mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoro Mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}. == Cultural Properties == ; Okayama Prefecture–designated Important Cultural Properties * The Nakazato Fujizuka (mound) – one of the fujizuka built by the Fuji-kō group (pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. In Kurashiki it is one of the larger mounds, with a height of 12 meters from its southern base and a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument dated to 1822 (Bunsei 5) indicates that its construction began in the Edo period. Among 36 stone structures, its dōsojin monument is unique in the district. ; Treasures * Ema (votive plaques) depicting the subjugation of the Yamata no Orochi (dating from Tenpō 5, July) * Ema commemorating a Fuji ascent (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting a pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)＜ref＞{{Cite book|和書|author=Ishikamin Library Local Materials Section|title=Ema of Nerima|publisher=Nerima City Board of Education|year=1978|page=175,178}}＜/ref＞ == Address == * 2855 Kojima Yuka, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Setchu Chūō Expressway}} at {{ill|Mizushima IC|ja|水島IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水島インターチェンジ|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}}, take {{ill|Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja|岡山児島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道21号岡山児島線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472422}} east toward Okayama, and at the “Gōnai” intersection turn east onto {{ill|Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja|玉野福田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道62号玉野福田線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 62|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472679}}. Continue straight along the route – about 15 minutes by car. * By train: Approximately a 15‑minute taxi ride from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ohashi Line}}. * By bus: A “Shimoden” bus (downward bus) runs from Kojima Station; however, there are only two round-trips per day (one in the morning, one in the evening) with a stop time of about 5 minutes. (Buses do not run on Saturdays, Sundays, holidays, or the New Year’s three days.) == Adjacent Sacred Sites == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkai-in|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-in|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} – '''6 Yugasan Rendaiji''' – 7 {{ill|Entsū-ji|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsū-ji|1=Entsū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Kurashiki High School Educational Research Group, Social Studies Division, History Section|title=New Edition: Historical Strolls of Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppan|year=1991|pages=96–97}} * Local explanatory plaques == References == {{Reflist|group=2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuka Shrine Main|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yoshida Yoshi-dō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Yoshi-dō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} – credited with painting the 50‐faced barrier paintings at Rendaiji. == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Yugasan Rendaiji Official Website] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Properties Protection Division] {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] }} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621649]] Wikidata name: Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rendaiji | image = [[File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall | Location = 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture | Latitude = 34|lat_m = 30|lat_s = 20.25 | Longitude = 133|long_m = 51|long_s = 2.5 | Mountain Name = {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}} | Sect = {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | Rank = Special Head Temple | Main image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}
{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} | Founded Year = Traditionally {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 10) | Founder = Traditionally {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} | Formal Name = Yuga-san Yuga Daigongen Rendaiji | Cultural Properties = Guest Hall, Tahōtō Pagoda, Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen, Bonshō (Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture)
Bronze Swords (Important Cultural Property of Kurashiki City) | Map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Rendaiji''' (蓮台寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, classified as a Special Head Temple. Its mountain name is {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The main images are {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}} and {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}. It is the 6th temple in the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and the 8th in the {{ill|108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == According to tradition, Rendaiji was founded in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} (Tenpyō 10) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, who enshrined Eleven-Faced Kannon as the main deity alongside {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}, manifestations of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}}.[https://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html History of Yuga-san Rendaiji] The temple declined after being ravaged by fire in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, but it was restored during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}} by the monk Zōun. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it was under the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} in {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}. {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} had been a focal point of faith for centuries, but the temple was divided following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} policy of the early {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治初年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}}, with the grounds allocated to form {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}} (later Yuga Shrine Main Shrine). After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}} and the dissolution of State Shinto, the temple and shrine operated de facto as a single entity, although formally separate. In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} (Heisei 9), disputes arose when Yuga Shrine resumed independent religious activities, leading to a legal battle over property rights. These conflicts were attributed to disagreements between the head priest of Rendaiji and his brother, the chief priest of Yuga Shrine. The custom of {{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—visiting both Yuga Daigongen and {{ill|Kotohira-gū|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}})—was popular during the Edo Period. == Overview == Rendaiji is revered as a temple for warding off misfortunes. In {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} (Heisei 19), Japan's tallest wooden statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} at 7.59 meters was completed.[https://yugasan.jp/19nen.html Yuga-san Rendaiji - Yakuyoke Fudō] The a11roach to Yuga Mountain is lined with a {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monzen-machi|qq=}}, with {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} as its specialty. == Cultural Properties == * '''Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture''' ''' Guest Hall ''' Tahōtō Pagoda ''' Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5428.htm Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen - Kurashiki City] - Stylistically resembling the works of {{ill|Kaihō Yūshō|en|Kaihō Yūshō|WD=Q3191951|lt=Kaihō Yūshō}} and {{ill|Kanō Sanraku|en|Kanō Sanraku|WD=Q3064625|lt=Kanō Sanraku}}, this screen showcases the vigor of pine trees and rock faces. ''' Bonshō (Temple Bell) * '''Important Cultural Properties of Kurashiki City''' ''' Bronze Swords == Location == * 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From {{ill|Mizushima Interchange|ja|水島インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima Interchange|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}} on the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Seto Chūō Expressway}}, follow Prefectural Route 21 (Okayama-Kojima Line) to the "Gōnai" intersection, then continue east on Prefectural Route 62. A11rox. 15 minutes. * By taxi: A11rox. 15 minutes from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ōhashi Line}}. * By bus: Limited bus service from Kojima Station to Yuga Mountain; not recommended for sightseeing due to infrequent schedules. == Pilgrimage Adjacency == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkaiin|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaiin|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} -* '''6 Rendaiji''' -- 7 {{ill|Entsūji (Kurashiki)|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsūji (Kurashiki)|1=Entsūji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == References == * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Society, History Division, ed. (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Yugasan Rendaiji''' ({{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}}) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Mimuro Sect|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Mimuro Sect|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} located in the Kojima area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is classified as an Extraordinary Head Temple. Its mountain name is Yuka-yama (由加山), and its principal images are the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu and Yogācāra Daigongen. It is pilgrimage site No. 6 on the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and No. 8 on the {{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == Tradition holds that in the 10th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō Era}} (738), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} enshrined the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu (the principal image) along with Yogācāra Daigongen (a manifestation of the trace of Amida and Yakushi) and thus founded the temple. Although the temple declined after being damaged by warfare at the end of the Heian period, it was revived during the Muromachi period by a high-Ranking monk. In the Edo period it received the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Han}} of Bizen Province. Long revered for its Yogācāra Daigongen, following the Meiji [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] edict, a separate shrine – later known as {{ill|Yuka Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} – was established within the temple grounds. After the post–World War II dissolution of State Shinto, although formally separated, the monastic staff continued to perform rituals in the main hall so that, in practice, the pre–Meiji state of affairs was restored. As a result, while Yuka Shrine retained its legal status as a separate religious corporation and maintained its own priests, it was in effect nearly dormant. In 1997 (Heisei 9) on March 3, Yuka Shrine began independent religious activities by occupying part of the temple buildings. Since then Rendaiji and Yuka Shrine have been in conflict—with Rendaiji seeking the return of its buildings—though Yuka Shrine continues its independent operations. A traditional custom called “Ryōmairi” (simultaneous pilgrimage to both shrines) developed in the Edo period in which pilgrims visited both the Yogācāra Daigongen of Yugasan and the {{ill|Kinpirō Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpirō Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現|1=Kompira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167077}} (now at {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kintōhira-gū}}) in Sanuki Province. == Overview == It is widely venerated as a protective deity against misfortune. In 2007 (Heisei 19) a 7.59‑meter tall wooden statue of the immovable 【ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王】—the tallest of its kind in Japan—was completed. The approach (sando) up Yuka-yama is lined by a traditional town (monzenchō) whose local specialty is the sweet rice cake known as {{ill|Ankoro Mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoro Mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}. == Cultural Properties == ; Okayama Prefecture–designated Important Cultural Properties * The Nakazato Fujizuka (mound) – one of the fujizuka built by the Fuji-kō group (pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. In Kurashiki it is one of the larger mounds, with a height of 12 meters from its southern base and a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument dated to 1822 (Bunsei 5) indicates that its construction began in the Edo period. Among 36 stone structures, its dōsojin monument is unique in the district. ; Treasures * Ema (votive plaques) depicting the subjugation of the Yamata no Orochi (dating from Tenpō 5, July) * Ema commemorating a Fuji ascent (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting a pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)＜ref＞{{Cite book|和書|author=Ishikamin Library Local Materials Section|title=Ema of Nerima|publisher=Nerima City Board of Education|year=1978|page=175,178}}＜/ref＞ == Address == * 2855 Kojima Yuka, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Setchu Chūō Expressway}} at {{ill|Mizushima IC|ja|水島IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水島インターチェンジ|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}}, take {{ill|Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja|岡山児島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道21号岡山児島線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472422}} east toward Okayama, and at the “Gōnai” intersection turn east onto {{ill|Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja|玉野福田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道62号玉野福田線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 62|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472679}}. Continue straight along the route – about 15 minutes by car. * By train: Approximately a 15‑minute taxi ride from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ohashi Line}}. * By bus: A “Shimoden” bus (downward bus) runs from Kojima Station; however, there are only two round-trips per day (one in the morning, one in the evening) with a stop time of about 5 minutes. (Buses do not run on Saturdays, Sundays, holidays, or the New Year’s three days.) == Adjacent Sacred Sites == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkai-in|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-in|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} – '''6 Yugasan Rendaiji''' – 7 {{ill|Entsū-ji|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsū-ji|1=Entsū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Kurashiki High School Educational Research Group, Social Studies Division, History Section|title=New Edition: Historical Strolls of Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppan|year=1991|pages=96–97}} * Local explanatory plaques == References == {{Reflist|group=2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuka Shrine Main|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yoshida Yoshi-dō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Yoshi-dō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} – credited with painting the 50‐faced barrier paintings at Rendaiji. == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Yugasan Rendaiji Official Website] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Properties Protection Division] {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] }} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621649]] Wikidata name: Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rendaiji | image = [[File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall | Location = 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture | Latitude = 34|lat_m = 30|lat_s = 20.25 | Longitude = 133|long_m = 51|long_s = 2.5 | Mountain Name = {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}} | Sect = {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | Rank = Special Head Temple | Main image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}
{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} | Founded Year = Traditionally {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 10) | Founder = Traditionally {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} | Formal Name = Yuga-san Yuga Daigongen Rendaiji | Cultural Properties = Guest Hall, Tahōtō Pagoda, Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen, Bonshō (Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture)
Bronze Swords (Important Cultural Property of Kurashiki City) | Map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Rendaiji''' (蓮台寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, classified as a Special Head Temple. Its mountain name is {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The main images are {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}} and {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}. It is the 6th temple in the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and the 8th in the {{ill|108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == According to tradition, Rendaiji was founded in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} (Tenpyō 10) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, who enshrined Eleven-Faced Kannon as the main deity alongside {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}, manifestations of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}}.[https://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html History of Yuga-san Rendaiji] The temple declined after being ravaged by fire in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, but it was restored during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}} by the monk Zōun. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it was under the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} in {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}. {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} had been a focal point of faith for centuries, but the temple was divided following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} policy of the early {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治初年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}}, with the grounds allocated to form {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}} (later Yuga Shrine Main Shrine). After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}} and the dissolution of State Shinto, the temple and shrine operated de facto as a single entity, although formally separate. In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} (Heisei 9), disputes arose when Yuga Shrine resumed independent religious activities, leading to a legal battle over property rights. These conflicts were attributed to disagreements between the head priest of Rendaiji and his brother, the chief priest of Yuga Shrine. The custom of {{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—visiting both Yuga Daigongen and {{ill|Kotohira-gū|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}})—was popular during the Edo Period. == Overview == Rendaiji is revered as a temple for warding off misfortunes. In {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} (Heisei 19), Japan's tallest wooden statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} at 7.59 meters was completed.[https://yugasan.jp/19nen.html Yuga-san Rendaiji - Yakuyoke Fudō] The a11roach to Yuga Mountain is lined with a {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monzen-machi|qq=}}, with {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} as its specialty. == Cultural Properties == * '''Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture''' ''' Guest Hall ''' Tahōtō Pagoda ''' Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5428.htm Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen - Kurashiki City] - Stylistically resembling the works of {{ill|Kaihō Yūshō|en|Kaihō Yūshō|WD=Q3191951|lt=Kaihō Yūshō}} and {{ill|Kanō Sanraku|en|Kanō Sanraku|WD=Q3064625|lt=Kanō Sanraku}}, this screen showcases the vigor of pine trees and rock faces. ''' Bonshō (Temple Bell) * '''Important Cultural Properties of Kurashiki City''' ''' Bronze Swords == Location == * 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From {{ill|Mizushima Interchange|ja|水島インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima Interchange|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}} on the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Seto Chūō Expressway}}, follow Prefectural Route 21 (Okayama-Kojima Line) to the "Gōnai" intersection, then continue east on Prefectural Route 62. A11rox. 15 minutes. * By taxi: A11rox. 15 minutes from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ōhashi Line}}. * By bus: Limited bus service from Kojima Station to Yuga Mountain; not recommended for sightseeing due to infrequent schedules. == Pilgrimage Adjacency == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkaiin|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaiin|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} -* '''6 Rendaiji''' -- 7 {{ill|Entsūji (Kurashiki)|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsūji (Kurashiki)|1=Entsūji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == References == * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Society, History Division, ed. ''New Edition: History Walks of Okayama Prefecture.'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, 11. 96-97. * On-site informational panels. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki, Hayashi)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki, Hayashi)|1=Kumano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568940}} * {{ill|Shibata Gidō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Gidō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} - Painted 50 sliding door murals at Rendaiji. == External Links == {{Commons|Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Official site of Yuga-san Rendaiji] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Heritage Protection Section] {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{wikidata link|Q11621649}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Gyōki|伝]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu Shūgō]] [[Category:Temples of the Shingon Omuro branch]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuga faith]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)]][[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)]][[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)]][[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Omuro school|WD=Q420739}}|prefecture={{ill|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.505625|N|133.85069444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yugasan Rendaiji | native_name = {{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} | image = [[File:{{ill|Rendaiji Main Hall|ja|蓮台寺 01|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.jpg|270px]] | image description = Main Hall (Sōhonden) | address = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, Kojima Yuka 2855 | latitude = 34 | latitude minutes = 30 | latitude seconds = 20.25 | longitude = 133 | longitude minutes = 51 | longitude seconds = 2.5 | mountain_name = {{ill|Yuka-yama|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka-yama|1=Yuga Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577570}} | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism, Mimuro-ha|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism, Mimuro-ha|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | temple_Ranking = Classified as an “Extraordinary Head Temple” | main_image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}{{ill|Yogācāra Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yogācāra Daigongen|1=Yuga Daigongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573853}} | founded = Tradition: Year 3 of {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daibō Era}} (703) | founder = | official_name = Yuka-yama Yogācāra Daigongen Rendaiji | alternate_names = | pilgrimage = {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No. 6{{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 8 | cultural_properties = Guest Hall, Tahō Tower, Kikujidō Illustrated Folding Screen, and the Bell (Designated as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property)Copper Sword (Designated as a City Important Cultural Property) | map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Yugasan Rendaiji''' ({{ill|Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji|1=rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621648}}) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Mimuro Sect|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Mimuro Sect|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} located in the Kojima area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is classified as an Extraordinary Head Temple. Its mountain name is Yuka-yama (由加山), and its principal images are the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu and Yogācāra Daigongen. It is pilgrimage site No. 6 on the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and No. 8 on the {{ill|Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuhachi Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == Tradition holds that in the 10th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō Era}} (738), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} enshrined the Eleven-faced Kannon Bosatsu (the principal image) along with Yogācāra Daigongen (a manifestation of the trace of Amida and Yakushi) and thus founded the temple. Although the temple declined after being damaged by warfare at the end of the Heian period, it was revived during the Muromachi period by a high-Ranking monk. In the Edo period it received the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Han}} of Bizen Province. Long revered for its Yogācāra Daigongen, following the Meiji [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] edict, a separate shrine – later known as {{ill|Yuka Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} – was established within the temple grounds. After the post–World War II dissolution of State Shinto, although formally separated, the monastic staff continued to perform rituals in the main hall so that, in practice, the pre–Meiji state of affairs was restored. As a result, while Yuka Shrine retained its legal status as a separate religious corporation and maintained its own priests, it was in effect nearly dormant. In 1997 (Heisei 9) on March 3, Yuka Shrine began independent religious activities by occupying part of the temple buildings. Since then Rendaiji and Yuka Shrine have been in conflict—with Rendaiji seeking the return of its buildings—though Yuka Shrine continues its independent operations. A traditional custom called “Ryōmairi” (simultaneous pilgrimage to both shrines) developed in the Edo period in which pilgrims visited both the Yogācāra Daigongen of Yugasan and the {{ill|Kinpirō Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpirō Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現|1=Kompira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q167077}} (now at {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kintōhira-gū}}) in Sanuki Province. == Overview == It is widely venerated as a protective deity against misfortune. In 2007 (Heisei 19) a 7.59‑meter tall wooden statue of the immovable 【ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王】—the tallest of its kind in Japan—was completed. The approach (sando) up Yuka-yama is lined by a traditional town (monzenchō) whose local specialty is the sweet rice cake known as {{ill|Ankoro Mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoro Mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}. == Cultural Properties == ; Okayama Prefecture–designated Important Cultural Properties * The Nakazato Fujizuka (mound) – one of the fujizuka built by the Fuji-kō group (pilgrims to Mt. Fuji) from the Edo period to early Showa period. In Kurashiki it is one of the larger mounds, with a height of 12 meters from its southern base and a diameter of 30 meters. A stone monument dated to 1822 (Bunsei 5) indicates that its construction began in the Edo period. Among 36 stone structures, its dōsojin monument is unique in the district. ; Treasures * Ema (votive plaques) depicting the subjugation of the Yamata no Orochi (dating from Tenpō 5, July) * Ema commemorating a Fuji ascent (dating from Keien 7, June) * Ema depicting a pilgrimage to Ise (by Fujigoro and Murata Masatarō, dating from Keien 3, March)＜ref＞{{Cite book|和書|author=Ishikamin Library Local Materials Section|title=Ema of Nerima|publisher=Nerima City Board of Education|year=1978|page=175,178}}＜/ref＞ == Address == * 2855 Kojima Yuka, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Setchu Chūō Expressway}} at {{ill|Mizushima IC|ja|水島IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水島インターチェンジ|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}}, take {{ill|Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja|岡山児島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Kojima Road No.21|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道21号岡山児島線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472422}} east toward Okayama, and at the “Gōnai” intersection turn east onto {{ill|Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja|玉野福田線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Tamano Fukuda Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山県道62号玉野福田線|1=Okayama Prefectural Road Route 62|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472679}}. Continue straight along the route – about 15 minutes by car. * By train: Approximately a 15‑minute taxi ride from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ohashi Line}}. * By bus: A “Shimoden” bus (downward bus) runs from Kojima Station; however, there are only two round-trips per day (one in the morning, one in the evening) with a stop time of about 5 minutes. (Buses do not run on Saturdays, Sundays, holidays, or the New Year’s three days.) == Adjacent Sacred Sites == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=China 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkai-in|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkai-in|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} – '''6 Yugasan Rendaiji''' – 7 {{ill|Entsū-ji|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsū-ji|1=Entsū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Kurashiki High School Educational Research Group, Social Studies Division, History Section|title=New Edition: Historical Strolls of Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppan|year=1991|pages=96–97}} * Local explanatory plaques == References == {{Reflist|group=2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuka Shrine Main|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yoshida Yoshi-dō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Yoshi-dō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} – credited with painting the 50‐faced barrier paintings at Rendaiji. == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Yugasan Rendaiji Official Website] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Properties Protection Division] {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] }} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:瑜伽山蓮台寺]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{Infobox Japanese Temple | Name = Rendaiji | image = [[File:Rendaiji 01.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall | Location = 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture | Latitude = 34|lat_m = 30|lat_s = 20.25 | Longitude = 133|long_m = 51|long_s = 2.5 | Mountain Name = {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}} | Sect = {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} | Rank = Special Head Temple | Main image = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}}
{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} | Founded Year = Traditionally {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 10) | Founder = Traditionally {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} | Formal Name = Yuga-san Yuga Daigongen Rendaiji | Cultural Properties = Guest Hall, Tahōtō Pagoda, Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen, Bonshō (Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture)
Bronze Swords (Important Cultural Property of Kurashiki City) | Map2 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=11|marker=religious-buddhist|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.5|frame-longitude=133.88}}{{Location map|Japan Okayama|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=12|label=Rendaiji|float=center}} }} '''Rendaiji''' (蓮台寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism|1=Omuro school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q420739}} in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, classified as a Special Head Temple. Its mountain name is {{ill|Yuga Mountain|ja|由加山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Mountain|qq=}}. The main images are {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon}} and {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}. It is the 6th temple in the {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} and the 8th in the {{ill|108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|百八観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/108 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == According to tradition, Rendaiji was founded in {{ill|738|en|738|WD=Q23585|lt=738 CE}} (Tenpyō 10) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, who enshrined Eleven-Faced Kannon as the main deity alongside {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}, manifestations of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha}} and {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}}.[https://www.yugasan.jp/engi.html History of Yuga-san Rendaiji] The temple declined after being ravaged by fire in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, but it was restored during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}} by the monk Zōun. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it was under the protection of the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}} in {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}. {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} had been a focal point of faith for centuries, but the temple was divided following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}} policy of the early {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治初年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}}, with the grounds allocated to form {{ill|Yuga Shrine|ja|由加神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine|qq=}} (later Yuga Shrine Main Shrine). After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}} and the dissolution of State Shinto, the temple and shrine operated de facto as a single entity, although formally separate. In {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}} (Heisei 9), disputes arose when Yuga Shrine resumed independent religious activities, leading to a legal battle over property rights. These conflicts were attributed to disagreements between the head priest of Rendaiji and his brother, the chief priest of Yuga Shrine. The custom of {{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—visiting both Yuga Daigongen and {{ill|Kotohira-gū|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}})—was popular during the Edo Period. == Overview == Rendaiji is revered as a temple for warding off misfortunes. In {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} (Heisei 19), Japan's tallest wooden statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} at 7.59 meters was completed.[https://yugasan.jp/19nen.html Yuga-san Rendaiji - Yakuyoke Fudō] The a11roach to Yuga Mountain is lined with a {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monzen-machi|qq=}}, with {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} as its specialty. == Cultural Properties == * '''Important Cultural Properties of Okayama Prefecture''' ''' Guest Hall ''' Tahōtō Pagoda ''' Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5428.htm Chrysanthemum Jidō Folding Screen - Kurashiki City] - Stylistically resembling the works of {{ill|Kaihō Yūshō|en|Kaihō Yūshō|WD=Q3191951|lt=Kaihō Yūshō}} and {{ill|Kanō Sanraku|en|Kanō Sanraku|WD=Q3064625|lt=Kanō Sanraku}}, this screen showcases the vigor of pine trees and rock faces. ''' Bonshō (Temple Bell) * '''Important Cultural Properties of Kurashiki City''' ''' Bronze Swords == Location == * 2855 Yuga, Kojima, Kurashiki, Okayama Prefecture == Access == * By car: From {{ill|Mizushima Interchange|ja|水島インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima Interchange|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129865}} on the {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|WD=Q11566530|lt=Seto Chūō Expressway}}, follow Prefectural Route 21 (Okayama-Kojima Line) to the "Gōnai" intersection, then continue east on Prefectural Route 62. A11rox. 15 minutes. * By taxi: A11rox. 15 minutes from {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|WD=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station}} on the {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|WD=Q7456750|lt=Seto Ōhashi Line}}. * By bus: Limited bus service from Kojima Station to Yuga Mountain; not recommended for sightseeing due to infrequent schedules. == Pilgrimage Adjacency == ; {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} : 5 {{ill|Hōkaiin|ja|法界院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkaiin|1=Hōkai-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555275}} -* '''6 Rendaiji''' -- 7 {{ill|Entsūji (Kurashiki)|ja|円通寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entsūji (Kurashiki)|1=Entsūji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10927538}} == References == * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Society, History Division, ed. ''New Edition: History Walks of Okayama Prefecture.'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, 11. 96-97. * On-site informational panels. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}} * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki, Hayashi)|ja|熊野神社 (倉敷市林)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Kurashiki, Hayashi)|1=Kumano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568940}} * {{ill|Shibata Gidō|ja|柴田義董|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Gidō|1=Shibata Gito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534904}} - Painted 50 sliding door murals at Rendaiji. == External Links == {{Commons|Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Rendaiji}} * [https://yugasan.jp/ Official site of Yuga-san Rendaiji] * [https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/bunkahogo/ Kurashiki City Cultural Heritage Protection Section] {{Yuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|version=104236529|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蓮台寺 (倉敷市)]] {{Commons category|Rendai-ji (Kurashiki)}} {{wikidata link|Q11621649}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Gyōki|伝]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu Shūgō]] [[Category:Temples of the Shingon Omuro branch]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuga faith]]
